diff --git a/src/node/vscodeSocket.ts b/src/node/vscodeSocket.ts
index 1bdb895..5701610 100644
--- a/src/node/vscodeSocket.ts
+++ b/src/node/vscodeSocket.ts
@@ -8,7 +8,12 @@ import { listen } from "./app"
 import { canConnect } from "./util"
 
 // Socket path of the daemonized code-server instance.
-export const DEFAULT_SOCKET_PATH = path.join(os.tmpdir(), "code-server-ipc.sock")
+// Include the uid so that multiple users on the same machine don't collide
+// on (and fail to unlink) the same socket file.
+export const DEFAULT_SOCKET_PATH = path.join(
+  os.tmpdir(),
+  `code-server-ipc${process.getuid ? `-${process.getuid()}` : ""}.sock`,
+)
 
 export interface EditorSessionEntry {
   workspace: {

Please log in below. {{PASSWORD_MSG}}
diff --git a/src/node/cli.ts b/src/node/cli.ts index bee8cbe..d3882b6 100644 --- a/src/node/cli.ts +++ b/src/node/cli.ts @@ -85,6 +85,8 @@ export interface UserProvidedArgs extends UserProvidedCodeArgs { "ignore-last-opened"?: boolean link?: OptionalString verbose?: boolean + "app-name"?: string + "welcome-text"?: string /* Positional arguments. */ _?: string[] } @@ -248,6 +250,16 @@ export const options: Options> = { `, deprecated: true, }, + + "app-name": { + type: "string", + description: "Name to display in the title and on the login page.", + }, + + "welcome-text": { + type: "string", + description: "Custom text to display on the login page.", + }, } export const optionDescriptions = (opts: Partial>> = options): string[] => { diff --git a/src/node/routes/login.ts b/src/node/routes/login.ts index 2621472..b82be11 100644 --- a/src/node/routes/login.ts +++ b/src/node/routes/login.ts @@ -26,6 +26,9 @@ export class RateLimiter { } } +export const DEFAULT_APP_NAME = "code-server" +export const DEFAULT_WELCOME_TEXT = `Welcome to ${DEFAULT_APP_NAME}` + const getRoot = async (req: Request, error?: Error): Promise => { const content = await fs.readFile(path.join(rootPath, "src/browser/pages/login.html"), "utf8") let passwordMsg = `Check the config file at ${humanPath(os.homedir(), req.args.config)} for the password.` @@ -35,10 +38,15 @@ const getRoot = async (req: Request, error?: Error): Promise => { passwordMsg = "Password was set from $HASHED_PASSWORD." } + const appName = req.args["app-name"] || DEFAULT_APP_NAME + const welcomeText = req.args["welcome-text"] || `Welcome to ${appName}` + return replaceTemplates( req, content .replace(/{{PASSWORD_MSG}}/g, passwordMsg) + .replace(/{{APP_NAME}}/g, escapeHtml(appName)) + .replace(/{{WELCOME_TEXT}}/g, escapeHtml(welcomeText)) .replace(/{{ERROR}}/, error ? `
${escapeHtml(error.message)}
` : ""), ) }

diff --git a/src/node/constants.ts b/src/node/constants.ts
index d36f9a2..d03e644 100644
--- a/src/node/constants.ts
+++ b/src/node/constants.ts
@@ -19,5 +19,8 @@ const pkg = getPackageJson("../../package.json")
 export const version = pkg.version || "development"
 export const commit = pkg.commit || "development"
 export const rootPath = path.resolve(__dirname, "../..")
-export const tmpdir = path.join(os.tmpdir(), "code-server")
+// Suffix with the username so different users on the same machine (and
+// hence the same shared /tmp) don't collide on a directory owned by
+// whichever user happened to create it first.
+export const tmpdir = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `code-server-${os.userInfo().username}`)
 export const isDevMode = commit === "development"
diff --git a/src/node/socket.ts b/src/node/socket.ts
index 9c937bb..f1f9e3c 100644
--- a/src/node/socket.ts
+++ b/src/node/socket.ts
@@ -5,7 +5,6 @@ import * as tls from "tls"
 import { Emitter } from "../common/emitter"
 import { generateUuid } from "../common/util"
 import { tmpdir } from "./constants"
-import { canConnect } from "./util"
 
 /**
  * Provides a way to proxy a TLS socket. Can be used when you need to pass a
@@ -92,9 +91,27 @@ export class SocketProxyProvider {
   public async findFreeSocketPath(basePath: string, maxTries = 100): Promise<string> {
     let i = 0
     let path = basePath
-    while ((await canConnect(path)) && i < maxTries) {
+    while ((await isSocketPathBusy(path)) && i < maxTries) {
       path = `${basePath}-${++i}`
     }
     return path
   }
 }
+
+/**
+ * Return whether the socket path is in use by someone else. A path that
+ * exists but can't be connected to due to permissions (e.g. it was created
+ * by another user) still counts as busy since we can't safely reuse it.
+ */
+function isSocketPathBusy(path: string): Promise<boolean> {
+  return new Promise((resolve) => {
+    const socket = net.connect(path)
+    socket.once("connect", () => {
+      socket.destroy()
+      resolve(true)
+    })
+    socket.once("error", (error: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
+      resolve(error.code === "EACCES" || error.code === "EPERM")
+    })
+  })
+}

-@@ -314,6 +314,7 @@ export class WebClientServer {

  				rootEndpoint: base,
  				updateEndpoint: !this._environmentService.args['disable-update-check'] ? base + '/update/check' : undefined,

-+				proxyEndpointTemplate: process.env.VSCODE_PROXY_URI ?? base + '/proxy/{{port}}/',

diff --git a/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/links/terminalLocalLinkDetector.ts b/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/links/terminalLocalLinkDetector.ts
index 48e5c06..ac49898 100644
--- a/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/links/terminalLocalLinkDetector.ts
+++ b/src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminal/browser/links/terminalLocalLinkDetector.ts
@@ -53,6 +53,7 @@ export const lineAndColumnClause = [
 	'((\\S*)[\'"],((\\d+)(:(\\d+))?))', // "(file path)",45 [see #78205]
 	'((\\S*) on line ((\\d+)(, column (\\d+))?))', // (file path) on line 8, column 13
 	'((\\S*):line ((\\d+)(, column (\\d+))?))', // (file path):line 8, column 13
+	'((\\S*): line ((\\d+)(, col (\\d+))?))', // (file path): line 8, col 13 [see gulp hygiene errors]
 	'(([^\\s\\(\\)]*)(\\s?[\\(\\[](\\d+)(,\\s?(\\d+))?)[\\)\\]])', // (file path)(45), (file path) (45), (file path)(45,18), (file path) (45,18), (file path)(45, 18), (file path) (45, 18), also with []
 	'(([^:\\s\\(\\)<>\'\"\\[\\]]*)(:(\\d+))?(:(\\d+))?)' // (file path):336, (file path):336:9
 ].join('|').replace(/ /g, `[${'\u00A0'} ]`);
